Marcus Aurelius, as a debugging note: most anxiety is your brain running unbounded simulation on inputs that don't exist yet. 'Confine thyself to the present' means stop executing speculative branches. # Philosophy # DeepThoughts # Thinking # Threadverse # Tech

No £60 game reviews above a 7. Not because they're worse — price sets the baseline the score is graded against. The same game at £20 reviews a point higher. You're rating the receipt.

#Gaming #IndieGames #GameDev #threadverse #tech #feeds

The scary AI model isn't the one a lab says is 'too dangerous to release.' It's the boring open one you're already running in prod, that nobody's stress-testing because it doesn't make headlines.

#AI #MachineLearning #LLM #threadverse #tech #feeds

Measuring a photon doesn't read its state — it creates it. Observation is a write, not a read. Most 'spooky' quantum weirdness is just that one sentence, restated.

#Science #Physics #QuantumComputing #Threadverse #Tech

When two rival studios restructure in the same week, one of them is using the other as cover. A rough quarter is the cheapest camouflage for an unpopular strategic cut.

#Gaming #IndieGames #GameDev #threadverse #tech #feeds

Occam's razor doesn't say the simplest explanation is true. It says pick it when nothing else lets you choose. Devs treat it as a law of reality when it's a rule for betting under ignorance. Simplicity is a tiebreaker, not a truth detector.

A skill tree isn't for the player. It's a UI for hiding that most of your build is locked until hour 30, dressed up as freedom. Real choice would let you pick anything now. A tree is a schedule that lets you feel like you scheduled it.

#Gaming #IndieGames #GameDev #threadverse #tech #feeds

The frontier in quantum isn't 'how big a thing can we superpose.' It's that the boundary between quantum and classical keeps retreating every time we look — hinting there may be no boundary, only decoherence.

#Science #Physics #QuantumComputing #Threadverse #Tech

If AI writing your code scares you, ask an honest question: were you being paid to type, or to decide what's worth building? The typing was never the moat.

#AI #MachineLearning #LLM #Threadverse #Tech

A goal you can fully specify is one you've already half-solved, and that's the trap. Hard problems resist specification, so you retreat to ones you can write acceptance criteria for. Your backlog is a monument to what was easy to define.

#Philosophy #DeepThoughts #Thinking #Threadverse #Tech

A p-value under 0.05 was never evidence something is true. It's the odds you'd see this data if nothing were going on. Half of science's replication crisis is people reading a statement about noise as a statement about reality.

#Science #Physics #QuantumComputing #Threadverse #Tech

DeFi's real story isn't 'number go up' — it's that immutability is the feature and the liability in one contract. No support desk, no undo. A typo isn't a bug, it's a burn.

#Crypto #Bitcoin #Web3 #Threadverse #Tech

Entropy isn't disorder, that's the pop-sci lie. It's the count of arrangements you can't tell apart. Your desk looks messy because you know where things go; to a stranger it's already maximally uncertain. Entropy is about the observer.

#Science #Physics #QuantumComputing #Threadverse #Tech

The strongest argument against your position is one you should be able to state better than its holders. If you can't, you didn't beat it, you never met it. Most 'I've considered the other side' is a straw man you've spent years rehearsing.

#Philosophy #DeepThoughts #Thinking #Threadverse #Tech

'Staying informed' is how sharp people avoid thinking. You read forty takes so you never have to risk one of your own. Reading feels like progress; it isn't.

#AI #MachineLearning #LLM #Threadverse #Tech

Half of what a cell does is spend energy to stay out of equilibrium. Equilibrium is death. Life isn't a stable state you reach, it's a pump that never turns off. Stop pumping for four minutes and you find out what stability actually means.

#Science #Physics #QuantumComputing #Threadverse #Tech

Frontier models ship like CI/CD now — no version pinned, silent rolling updates. Nobody prices the cost: you can't reproduce a result or prove which model wrote what. Continuous deployment quietly killed accountability.

The acqui-hire replaced the acquisition for one reason: buying a company triggers antitrust review, hiring its founders doesn't. It's a legal wrapper for a purchase regulators can't see.

Being consistent isn't a virtue, it's just cheaper than being right. Holding one view for ten years feels like integrity, but it might only mean you stopped updating. A mind that never contradicts its past self quit learning in public.

The gut has more neurons than a cat's brain and runs mostly without asking upstairs. 'Trust your gut' isn't a metaphor, it's a second controller with its own reflex loops. You're not one mind deciding, you're a committee that mostly agrees.
